name: Blades in the Dark
edition: Blades in the Dark Core (John Harper / Evil Hat / One Seven Design, 2017)
license: Original mechanics references; Blades in the Dark is published by John Harper / Evil Hat. The Forged in the Dark SRD is open-licensed; this bundle stays clear of trademarked Doskvol setting prose.
summary: Forged in the Dark heist game. Roll N d6 = Action rating ± modifiers; highest die determines outcome — 6 = success, 4-5 = partial, 1-3 = failure, multiple 6s = critical. GM declares Position (Controlled / Risky / Desperate) and Effect (Limited / Standard / Great) BEFORE the roll; both axes negotiate before dice hit the table.

dice.notation: Nd6 take highest (Action rating dice pool)

resolution.description: Blades rolls N d6 where N = Action rating (0-4) plus modifiers (assist +1, push yourself +1 for 2 stress, devil's bargain +1). The HIGHEST die determines the outcome: 6 = success, 4-5 = partial (you get it but with cost), 1-3 = failure. MULTIPLE 6s = critical (extra effect or no cost). Action 0 means rolling 2d6 and taking the WORST (desperate fumbling). The GM declares Position (Controlled / Risky / Desperate — sets consequence severity) AND Effect (Limited / Standard / Great — sets how much the success advances the goal) BEFORE the roll. Both axes are negotiable in fiction; pushing, devil's bargain, and assist can buy improvements at stress cost. The dice widget runs in manual NdX mode; the GM agent prompt teaches all the moves and consequences.
resolution.noticeText: Blades uses Nd6-take-highest with Position + Effect. The widget is manual — see gm-agent for full resolution flow.

attributes.name: Insight
attributes.description: Quick-thinking, perception, study. Covers Hunt / Study / Survey / Tinker Actions. Resists harm to mind / sense / cleverness — Insight resistance roll.

attributes.name: Prowess
attributes.description: Physical capability, stealth, combat. Covers Finesse / Prowl / Skirmish / Wreck Actions. Resists harm to body / position — Prowess resistance roll.

attributes.name: Resolve
attributes.description: Willpower, conviction, social presence. Covers Attune / Command / Consort / Sway Actions. Resists harm to morale / heart / will — Resolve resistance roll.

skills.name: Hunt
skills.description: Track, ambush, attack at range. Catching prey before they escape.

skills.name: Study
skills.description: Examine details, decipher meaning, recall lore. Reading a person or text.

skills.name: Survey
skills.description: Observe a situation, predict events, look around. Casing a place.

skills.name: Tinker
skills.description: Fiddle with devices, pick locks, disarm traps, modify gear.

skills.name: Finesse
skills.description: Sleight of hand, riding, controlling vehicles, dueling with quick precision.

skills.name: Prowl
skills.description: Sneak, hide, move quietly, parkour through unfamiliar terrain.

skills.name: Skirmish
skills.description: Engage in close combat, fight savagely, brawl. Up-close violence.

skills.name: Wreck
skills.description: Break things, demolish, sunder. Smashing through obstacles.

skills.name: Attune
skills.description: Sense, channel, or contact the ghost field. Resist spectral / electroplasmic threats.

skills.name: Command
skills.description: Intimidate, lead, demand obedience. Force someone to act against their preference.

skills.name: Consort
skills.description: Socialize, schmooze, network. Negotiate from a position of mutual interest.

skills.name: Sway
skills.description: Persuade, deceive, charm. Convince someone to see things your way.

Controlled.description: Position: you act from a position of advantage. Failure costs minor — withdraw, reduced Effect, a small complication.

Risky.description: Position: you act in the danger zone. Failure costs standard — harm, lost opportunity, escalating trouble.

Desperate.description: Position: you act under dire pressure. Failure costs severe — serious harm, lost objective, fatal complication.

Limited.description: Effect: even success advances the goal only slightly. Use when conditions or scale work against the PC.

Standard.description: Effect: success advances the goal as the player intended.

Great.description: Effect: success advances the goal further than expected — extra clock ticks, bonus information, broader impact.

derivedStats.name: Position
derivedStats.formula: GM-set per roll. Controlled / Risky / Desperate. Negotiable in fiction.

derivedStats.name: Effect
derivedStats.formula: GM-set per roll. Limited / Standard / Great. Negotiable.

vocabularyHints.pattern: I attack / I fight / I shoot
vocabularyHints.hint: In Blades: pick an Action rating (Skirmish for melee, Hunt for ranged, Finesse for fencing-style). Roll dice = rating. GM declares Position (Controlled/Risky/Desperate) and Effect (Limited/Standard/Great) BEFORE the roll. Highest die: 6 = success, 4-5 = partial (with cost), 1-3 = bad outcome.

vocabularyHints.pattern: I sneak / I hide / I prowl
vocabularyHints.hint: Prowl action. GM should declare Position based on guards / lighting / distance. Effect typically Standard unless circumstances are unusual.
Player gains +1d to the roll OR +1 Effect OR perform an action you couldn't otherwise (e.g. supernaturally hard feat). Cost: 2 Stress. Mark stress on the sheet.
GM offers a complication (collateral damage, double-crossed friend, attention drawn) in exchange for +1d. Player accepts or declines. If accepted, the complication WILL happen regardless of roll.

After taking a consequence (harm, lost ground, breakdown), the player rolls the appropriate Attribute (Insight/Prowess/Resolve) as Nd6 take highest. 6 = avoid it entirely; 4-5 = reduce it; 1-3 = take it. Cost: 6 - highest die in Stress (so a 6 = 0 stress, a 1 = 5 stress).

Downtime activity. Roll the lowest action rating in the Vice's category (or just 1d). Result = stress cleared. Watch for Overindulgence — rolling more dice than current stress can trigger a Vice complication.
